### Post by namaster on 2008-10-19
After installing them on DELL 6400 i cant get my screen to work. As soon as i login it gives me total white screen.

My xorg file. Any idea how to fix it?
```


# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]-0" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "Module"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver      "synaptics"
	Option	    "SendCoreEvents" "true"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/psaux"
	Option	    "Protocol" "auto-dev"
	Option	    "HorizEdgeScroll" "0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]-0"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]-0"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]-0"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]-0"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]-0"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection


```


aticonfig --initial -f
```

Uninitialised file found, configuring.
Using /etc/X11/xorg.conf
Saved back-up to /etc/X11/xorg.conf.fglrx-0
aticonfig: Writing to '/etc/X11/xorg.conf' failed. Bad file descriptor

```

```

display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: Mesa project: www.mesa3d.org
OpenGL renderer string: Mesa GLX Indirect
OpenGL version string: 1.4 (2.1 Mesa 7.0.3-rc2)

```

I can only see my screen through fail safe gnome session. Any idea how to fix it and enabled ati drivers?

### Post by Isilion on 2008-10-19
ATI driver sucks. everybody gots problems. Read this thread, it worked for me (follow Neo_The_User tips). At least it says that its properly installed, but launching 3d apps make system crash and i need to do a hardreboot.

Complain at ATI support. I think its a bad release driver (it cannot install by itself and make system incapable of login if you dont do some tricks).

Here's the thread: [http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=766699](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=766699)

### Post by Isilion on 2008-10-19
Namaster: type this:

fglrxinfo (enter) if it says anything different than ATI you should  do again installation.
first of all download envyng and use it to unistal ATI driver. that would put the default driver you got after ubuntu installation. then follow the steps of Neo_The_User, im sure that it works for all. Dont forget to edit the file mentioned and put "fglrx" between the DISABLED="".

---

### Post by namaster on 2008-10-19
> **Isilion said:**
> Namaster: type this:

fglrxinfo (enter) if it says anything different than ATI you should  do again installation.
first of all download envyng and use it to unistal ATI driver. that would put the default driver you got after ubuntu installation. then follow the steps of Neo_The_User, im sure that it works for all. Dont forget to edit the file mentioned and put "fglrx" between the DISABLED="".

Use to removed ATI using envyng but still ATI CC is their in application list. :P

Should this not install ATI completely?
```

cd /usr/share/ati
sudo sh ./fglrx-uninstall.sh

```

Still white screen after doing it again.
using fglrxinfo in safe gnome mode.
fglrxinfo
```

display: :0.0  screen: 0
OpenGL vendor string: Mesa project: www.mesa3d.org
OpenGL renderer string: Mesa GLX Indirect
OpenGL version string: 1.4 (2.1 Mesa 7.0.3-rc2)

```

Thanks for all the help.

---

### Post by namaster on 2008-10-19
Hurray!

was missing this:
```

sudo gedit /etc/modprobe.d/blacklist-restricted
sudo gedit /etc/modprobe.d/blacklist-local

```

Took me 4 hrs to figure that out lol..

---
